title: Java Call-In Exceptions
text: Java Call-In Exceptions Errors detected by Java methods are signaled by throwing exceptions. Table 1. Uniface Exceptions Exception Meaning NoSuchComponentException Thrown when designated component does not exist or could not be created. NoSuchOperationException Thrown when the designated component has no operation with the given name. UnifaceException Thrown for all error messages, except the two above. The text of the exception includes the Uniface error code. These exceptions are thrown at specific levels of the Java program. These levels are shown in the table: Table 2. Levels Where Exceptions are Thrown Level Exceptions thrown constructor NoSuchComponentException and UnifaceException activate NoSuchOperationException and UnifaceException remove , accept , exec , quit , and all user-defined methods. UnifaceException Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
